basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Ḥujurāt — “The Chambers” — is the sūra of high spiritual courtesy (adab): it teaches one to put nothing ahead of God and His Messenger, to lower the voice before the Prophet — for those who do are precisely “those whose hearts God has tested for godfearing.” It prescribes verifying news rather than acting on rumor, making peace between believers who quarrel, and forbids mockery, slander, suspicion, spying, and backbiting — “would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother?” Then comes the universal address: “O mankind, We created you… that you may know one another; the noblest before God is the most godfearing.” Qushayrī reads here that taqwā is liberation from the soul: the noblest servant is the furthest from himself and the nearest to God. It closes on the distinction between mere islām professed outwardly and the īmān that enters the heart: faith is the life of the heart, and the heart lives only after the slaughtering of the soul.
themes: adab before God and the Prophet
all created to know one another
professed islām and the īmān of the heart

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُقَدِّمُوا بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-ladhīna āmanū lā tuqaddimū bayna yadayi llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha samīʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not put yourselves forward before God and His Messenger! Fear God! Truly, God hears all and knows all.
versets.commentaire: The call “O you who believe!” is itself an honor: a testimony to the nobility of the one addressed. Then comes the charge: “do not go ahead.” Qushayrī observes that God sets the honor of the title before the burden of the duty. Not to go ahead is to decide nothing from one's own resources: “Halt where you have been placed, do what you have been commanded, and be people of following and faithfulness — not masters of innovation and self-beginning.”

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: address to the believers; per Qushayrī, a witness of honor to the one called.

mots.racine: ق د م
mots.def: qaddama: to put forward; decide nothing before God and His Messenger.

mots.racine: ي د ي / ر س ل
mots.def: idiom: in front of, ahead of.

mots.racine: و ق ي
س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: two divine Names: He hears all, knows all.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَرْفَعُوا أَصْوَاتَكُمْ فَوْقَ صَوْتِ النَّبِيِّ وَلَا تَجْهَرُوا لَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ كَجَهْرِ بَعْضِكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ أَن تَحْبَطَ أَعْمَالُكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-ladhīna āmanū lā tarfaʿū aṣwātakum fawqa ṣawti n-nabiyyi wa-lā tajharū lahu bi-l-qawli ka-jahri baʿḍikum li-baʿḍin an taḥbaṭa aʿmālukum wa-antum lā tashʿurūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, and do not speak loudly to him as you speak loudly to one another, lest your works become void without your being aware.
versets.commentaire: They are commanded to guard his inviolability and to observe courtesy in his service and company: not to look upon him with the eye by which one looks upon one's peers. If he is gentle and approachable in character, let them not for that take liberties or overstep their bounds. Qushayrī adds this subtlety: “Do not open speech with him first; wait until he opens it to you.”

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ص و ت
ف و ق / ن ب أ
mots.sens: above the voice of the Prophet

mots.racine: ج ه ر / ق و ل
mots.sens: do not speak loudly to him

mots.racine: ح ب ط / ع م ل
ش ع ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَغُضُّونَ أَصْوَاتَهُمْ عِندَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ امْتَحَنَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ ۚ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna l-ladhīna yaghuḍḍūna aṣwātahum ʿinda rasūli llāhi ulāʾika l-ladhīna mtaḥana llāhu qulūbahum li-t-taqwā lahum maghfiratun wa-ajrun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Those who lower their voices in the presence of His Messenger are truly those whose hearts God has tested for godfearing. They shall have forgiveness and a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: They are those upon whom the stillness (sakīna) descends from the awe of his presence. Qushayrī: God has tested their hearts for godfearing by tearing from them the love of passions; so they guarded against ill character and observed courtesy. And he adds, higher still: they are those who have stripped themselves of the habits of human nature.

mots.racine: غ ض ض / ص و ت
ع ن د / ر س ل
mots.sens: in the presence of God's Messenger

mots.racine: م ح ن / ق ل ب
و ق ي
غ ف ر / أ ج ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنَادُونَكَ مِن وَرَاءِ الْحُجُرَاتِ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: inna l-ladhīna yunādūnaka min warāʾi l-ḥujurāti aktharuhum lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: Those who call to you from outside your chambers are, for the most part, people who do not reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the reproach toward the knowledge of rank: had they known your measure, they would not have neglected your inviolability, but held to the awe of your dignity. To shout from behind the walls betrays blindness to the greatness of the one addressed. By contrast, he recalls the manner of the Companions who knew his rank: one of them would knock at his door with the tips of his fingernails.

mots.racine: ن د و
mots.def: nādā: to hail, call out loudly.

mots.racine: و ر ي / ح ج ر
mots.def: ḥujra (pl. ḥujurāt): chamber, apartment; warāʾ: behind.

mots.racine: ك ث ر
ع ق ل

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ صَبَرُوا حَتَّىٰ تَخْرُجَ إِلَيْهِمْ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-law annahum ṣabarū ḥattā takhruja ilayhim la-kāna khayran lahum wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Had they been patient until you came out to them, it would have been better for them. But God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had they been patient until you came out to them, without haste, without rousing you at the hour of midday rest by their calling, it would have been better for them. Indiscreet haste is its own punishment; patience at the threshold of the Beloved is already a portion of adab. And God, forgiving and compassionate, covers the heedlessness of the one who returns.

mots.racine: ص ب ر
خ ر ج
mots.sens: until you came out to them

mots.racine: ك و ن / خ ي ر
mots.sens: it would be better for them

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِن جَاءَكُمْ فَاسِقٌ بِنَبَإٍ فَتَبَيَّنُوا أَن تُصِيبُوا قَوْمًا بِجَهَالَةٍ فَتُصْبِحُوا عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلْتُمْ نَادِمِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-ladhīna āmanū in jāʾakum fāsiqun bi-nabaʾin fa-tabayyanū an tuṣībū qawman bi-jahālatin fa-tuṣbiḥū ʿalā mā faʿaltum nādimīna
versets.trad: O you who believe! If a corrupt person brings you news, verify it carefully; for if, out of ignorance, you were to harm certain people, you would have to repent of acting so.
versets.commentaire: The verse teaches not to rely on the word of the corrupt until its truth appears, and points to the refusal to listen to the calumniator, the talebearer, and the backbiter of people. The fāsiq, says Qushayrī, is one who has gone out of obedience; it is also said: one who has departed the bound of dignity; and again: one who has cast off the cloak of modesty.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ف س ق
mots.sens: if a corrupt one comes to you
mots.def: fāsiq: one gone out of obedience, depraved.

mots.racine: ن ب أ
ب ي ن
ص و ب / ج ه ل
mots.sens: lest you harm folk in ignorance
mots.def: aṣāba: to strike, hit; jahāla: ignorance.

mots.racine: ن د م

versets.ar: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ فِيكُمْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ۚ لَوْ يُطِيعُكُمْ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْرِ لَعَنِتُّمْ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَزَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ وَكَرَّهَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكُفْرَ وَالْفُسُوقَ وَالْعِصْيَانَ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الرَّاشِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿlamū anna fīkum rasūla llāhi law yuṭīʿukum fī kathīrin mina l-amri la-ʿanittum wa-lākinna llāha ḥabbaba ilaykumu l-īmāna wa-zayyanahu fī qulūbikum wa-karraha ilaykumu l-kufra wa-l-fusūqa wa-l-ʿiṣyāna ulāʾika humu r-rāshidūna
versets.trad: Know that the Messenger of God is among you. Were he to obey you in many matters, you would fall into hardship. But God has made faith dear to you and has adorned it in your hearts, and has made hateful to you disbelief, depravity, and disobedience. Those are the rightly guided,
versets.commentaire: Were the Messenger to follow you in most of your demands, you would fall into corruption (ʿanat). Qushayrī makes of this verse a proof: God has reserved for the believers graces (alṭāf) the disbelievers do not share; were He not to stir in them the motives of obedience, there would be neglect and shortcoming in worship. It is He who made faith beloved and adorned it in the hearts, and made its opposite hateful — pure favor from Him.

mots.racine: ر س ل
mots.sens: that God's Messenger is among you

mots.racine: ع ن ت
ح ب ب / أ م ن
mots.sens: He made faith dear to you

mots.racine: ز ي ن / ق ل ب
mots.sens: and adorned it in your hearts

mots.racine: ك ر ه / ك ف ر / ف س ق / ع ص ي
mots.def: karraha: to make odious; the three degrees of refusal.

mots.racine: ر ش د
mots.def: rāshid: one on the straight path.

versets.ar: فَضْلًا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَنِعْمَةً ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: faḍlan mina llāhi wa-niʿmatan wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: by the grace of God and His blessing. God is all-knowing, wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He did this in you as a favor from Himself and as a mercy. Right guidance is no acquisition of the servant but a gift: gratuitous favor (faḍl) and blessing (niʿma). And God is Knowing of who merits this election, Wise in the apportioning of His gifts.

mots.racine: ف ض ل
ن ع م
ع ل م / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَإِن طَائِفَتَانِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ اقْتَتَلُوا فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُمَا ۖ فَإِن بَغَتْ إِحْدَاهُمَا عَلَى الْأُخْرَىٰ فَقَاتِلُوا الَّتِي تَبْغِي حَتَّىٰ تَفِيءَ إِلَىٰ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن فَاءَتْ فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُمَا بِالْعَدْلِ وَأَقْسِطُوا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in ṭāʾifatāni mina l-muʾminīna qtatalū fa-aṣliḥū baynahumā fa-in baghat iḥdāhumā ʿalā l-ukhrā fa-qātilū llatī tabghī ḥattā tafīʾa ilā amri llāhi fa-in fāʾat fa-aṣliḥū baynahumā bi-l-ʿadli wa-aqsiṭū inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muqsiṭīna
versets.trad: If two groups of believers fight each other, reconcile them. And if one of them still rises against the other, fight the one that sows discord until it bows to God's command. If it bows, restore peace between the two groups with justice and equity. Truly, God loves those who are equitable!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws two legal lessons: the believer does not leave faith by his transgression — for of the two factions, one at least is inevitably at fault when they fight; and aiding the oppressed is obligatory. Then he interiorizes it: when the soul oppresses the heart by calling it to its passions and busying itself with its corruption, it must be fought with the swords of spiritual striving until it bends toward obedience; then it is spared, for it is the very mount that carries one to God's door.

mots.racine: ط و ف / أ م ن
ق ت ل
mots.def: iqtatala: to wage war on one another.

mots.racine: ص ل ح
ب غ ي
ف ي أ / أ م ر
mots.sens: until it returns to God's command

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ق س ط
mots.def: muqsiṭ: one who acts with equity.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَ أَخَوَيْكُمْ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: innamā l-muʾminūna ikhwatun fa-aṣliḥū bayna akhawaykum wa-ttaqū llāha laʿallakum turḥamūna
versets.trad: The believers are but brothers. So make peace between your brothers, and fear God! Perhaps you will be shown mercy...
versets.commentaire: Making peace between those who quarrel is among the most binding resolves of religion; and if that is obligatory, then the sin of the sower of discord and the talebearer is immense. Qushayrī unfolds the rights of brotherhood: do not force your brother to beg your help; do not neglect his needs until he must ask you for them; do not drive him to excuse himself, but find his excuse for him in advance; cover his fault, visit him when ill, keep his old pact, and watch over his kin in his presence and his absence, in his life and after his death.

mots.racine: أ م ن / أ خ و
ص ل ح / أ خ و
و ق ي
ر ح م
mots.sens: that you may be shown mercy

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا يَسْخَرْ قَوْمٌ مِّن قَوْمٍ عَسَىٰ أَن يَكُونُوا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُمْ وَلَا نِسَاءٌ مِّن نِّسَاءٍ عَسَىٰ أَن يَكُنَّ خَيْرًا مِّنْهُنَّ ۖ وَلَا تَلْمِزُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ وَلَا تَنَابَزُوا بِالْأَلْقَابِ ۖ بِئْسَ الِاسْمُ الْفُسُوقُ بَعْدَ الْإِيمَانِ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَتُبْ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-ladhīna āmanū lā yaskhar qawmun min qawmin ʿasā an yakūnū khayran minhum wa-lā nisāʾun min nisāʾin ʿasā an yakunna khayran minhunna wa-lā talmizū anfusakum wa-lā tanābazū bi-l-alqābi biʾsa l-ismu l-fusūqu baʿda l-īmāni wa-man lam yatub fa-ulāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Let no men mock other men; it may be that the latter are better than the mockers; nor women other women; it may be that the latter are better than the mockers. Do not defame one another, and do not call each other by nicknames. The insulting name is detestable among those who have received faith. Whoever does not repent of his faults is among the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: God forbids the contempt of people, backbiting, the slighting of rights, the abandonment of respect. “Do not defame yourselves”: let none of you disparage another — the other is as a part of oneself. Qushayrī: no one has belittled another but he was made to suffer in turn. Do not judge by the outward states of people, for in the corners lie hidden treasures, and the Real veils His friends in the cloak of lowliness — as the ḥadīth says: “Many a disheveled, dust-covered one in two rags, scorned, who, were he to swear by God, would have his oath fulfilled.”

mots.racine: س خ ر / ق و م
خ ي ر
mots.sens: perhaps they are better than them

mots.racine: ل م ز / ن ف س
ن ب ز / ل ق ب
mots.def: tanābaza: to call by epithets; laqab: nickname.

mots.racine: ب أ س / ف س ق
mots.sens: how evil is the name of depravity
mots.def: biʾsa: verb of blame; fusūq: iniquity.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اجْتَنِبُوا كَثِيرًا مِّنَ الظَّنِّ إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ إِثْمٌ ۖ وَلَا تَجَسَّسُوا وَلَا يَغْتَب بَّعْضُكُم بَعْضًا ۚ أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-ladhīna āmanū jtanibū kathīran mina ẓ-ẓanni inna baʿḍa ẓ-ẓanni ithmun wa-lā tajassasū wa-lā yaghtab baʿḍukum baʿḍan a-yuḥibbu aḥadukum an yaʾkula laḥma akhīhi maytan fa-karihtumūhu wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha tawwābun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Avoid much conjecture about others: some conjectures are sins. Do not spy on one another! Do not backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother? No, you would loathe it! Fear God! Truly, God accepts repentance, He is compassionate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī finely distinguishes the soul and the heart: the soul (nafs) does not speak truth, the heart does not lie; and to discern between them is hard. As long as the least portion of his cravings remains in a man, he cannot claim the pure speech of the heart, and he must suspect his soul in every fault he perceives in another. On spying: the knower of God does not turn from contemplating the Real toward contemplating creatures — how would he spy on their states, he who is not even distracted by his own self? And on backbiting: one backbites creatures only through absence from the Real.

mots.racine: ج ن ب / ظ ن ن
ظ ن ن / أ ث م
ج س س
غ ي ب
mots.def: ightāba: to speak ill of the absent.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ل ح م / م و ت
mots.sens: to eat the flesh of his dead brother

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَاكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَاكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَائِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوا ۚ إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ اللَّهِ أَتْقَاكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu innā khalaqnākum min dhakarin wa-unthā wa-jaʿalnākum shuʿūban wa-qabāʾila li-taʿārafū inna akramakum ʿinda llāhi atqākum inna llāha ʿalīmun khabīrun
versets.trad: O mankind! We created you from a male and a female. We made you into peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Truly, the noblest of you before God is the most godfearing. God is all-knowing, all-aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We created you all from Adam and Eve, then made you peoples and tribes to know one another — not to vie or boast. If origins are but earth, a drop, a clot, in what is there to boast? In molded clay? In the drop within a hidden lodging? In your deeds mixed with show, your states tinged with self-conceit? And he defines true nobility: “the noblest is the most godfearing” — that is, the furthest from his soul: taqwā is liberation from the soul, its cravings and its portions. The noblest servant is therefore the furthest from himself and the nearest to God.

mots.racine: ن و س
خ ل ق / ذ ك ر / أ ن ث
mots.sens: We created you from a male and a female

mots.racine: ش ع ب / ق ب ل
ع ر ف
mots.sens: that you may know one another
mots.def: taʿārafa: to come to mutual acquaintance.

mots.racine: ك ر م / و ق ي
mots.sens: the noblest is the most godfearing

versets.ar: قَالَتِ الْأَعْرَابُ آمَنَّا ۖ قُل لَّمْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَلَٰكِن قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنَا وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ الْإِيمَانُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ لَا يَلِتْكُم مِّنْ أَعْمَالِكُمْ شَيْئًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: qālati l-aʿrābu āmannā qul lam tuʾminū wa-lākin qūlū aslamnā wa-lammā yadkhuli l-īmānu fī qulūbikum wa-in tuṭīʿū llāha wa-rasūlahu lā yalitkum min aʿmālikum shayʾan inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: The desert Arabs say: “We believe!” Say to them: “You do not believe, but say rather: ‘We have embraced Islam,’ for faith has not yet entered your hearts. If you obey God and His Messenger, God will not deprive you of anything of your [good] deeds. God is forgiving, compassionate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse the spiritual heart of the sūra: faith is the life of the heart, and the heart lives only after the slaughtering of the soul. Yet souls do not die: they absent themselves; and while they are present, no good is accomplished. The submission of the outward is islām; but not everyone outwardly submitted is sincere in his secret. “Faith has not yet entered your hearts”: proof, he says, that the seat of faith is the heart — as the disease of the hypocrites lay in their hearts, and heart-disease and faith are two opposites.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ع ر ب / أ م ن
ق و ل / أ م ن
س ل م
د خ ل / أ م ن / ق ل ب
mots.sens: faith has not yet entered your hearts
mots.def: dakhala: to enter; the seat of faith: the heart.

mots.racine: ل ي ت / ع م ل
mots.sens: He will not diminish any of your works

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَرْتَابُوا وَجَاهَدُوا بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الصَّادِقُونَ
versets.tr: innamā l-muʾminūna l-ladhīna āmanū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi thumma lam yartābū wa-jāhadū bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim fī sabīli llāhi ulāʾika humu ṣ-ṣādiqūna
versets.trad: The true believers are only those who believe in God and His Messenger, then never doubt afterward, and who struggle with their wealth and their persons in the way of God. Those are the truthful.
versets.commentaire: God made faith conditional upon stated traits, sealed by the restrictive particle innamā which demands the converse: whoever falls short of these conditions set for faith, his profession of it is rejected. Qushayrī adds this equation: faith (īmān) brings the servant security (amān); where faith does not bring security, its holder is in greater peril than any other.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ر س ل
mots.sens: they believed in God and His Messenger
mots.def: āmana bi: to have faith in.

mots.racine: ر ي ب
ج ه د / م و ل / ن ف س
mots.sens: struggled with their wealth and persons

mots.racine: س ب ل
ص د ق

versets.ar: قُلْ أَتُعَلِّمُونَ اللَّهَ بِدِينِكُمْ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qul a-tuʿallimūna llāha bi-dīnikum wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Say: “Would you teach God your religion, when God knows what is in the heavens and on the earth, and God knows all things perfectly?”
versets.commentaire: How can one claim to instruct God in one's own religion when He knows all of the heavens and the earth? Qushayrī draws from it a rule of the adab of knowledge: one must halt (al-waqf) at the matters of religion as they are received: the names are taken from Him, the rulings sought from Him, and His commands are to be followed. Following is obligatory, innovation rejected.

mots.racine: ع ل م / د ي ن
mots.sens: would you teach God your religion

mots.racine: ع ل م / س م و
mots.sens: He knows what is in the heavens

mots.racine: أ ر ض
mots.sens: and what is on the earth

mots.racine: ك ل ل / ش ي أ / ع ل م

versets.ar: يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا ۖ قُل لَّا تَمُنُّوا عَلَيَّ إِسْلَامَكُم ۖ بَلِ اللَّهُ يَمُنُّ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْ هَدَاكُمْ لِلْإِيمَانِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: yamunnūna ʿalayka an aslamū qul lā tamunnū ʿalayya islāmakum bali llāhu yamunnu ʿalaykum an hadākum li-l-īmāni in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: They count it a favor to you that they have embraced Islam. Say to them: “Do not count your Islam as a favor to me. No! It is God who has done you a favor by guiding you to faith, if you are truthful!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dismantles the servant's claim to take credit for his acts: whoever regards one of his deeds — if he sees it as coming from himself, that is association (shirk); if he sees it for himself, that is hidden cunning. How then take credit for what is shirk or cunning? His duty is to receive the favor, not to deem himself its author over another. And he distinguishes: the favor (minna) of creatures sullies the kindness, but coming from God, it is by it that grace becomes pure and good.

mots.racine: م ن ن / س ل م
mots.sens: they reckon their submission a favor to you
mots.def: manna ʿalā: to claim credit for a benefit.

mots.racine: ق و ل / م ن ن / س ل م
mots.sens: say: do not count your islām a favor to me

mots.racine: م ن ن
ه د ي / أ م ن
ص د ق

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: inna llāha yaʿlamu ghayba s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-llāhu baṣīrun bi-mā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Truly, God knows the hidden of the heavens and the earth, and God sees perfectly what you do.
versets.commentaire: The sūra is sealed on the knowledge of the ghayb — the Unseen of the heavens and the earth — and on the divine gaze that encompasses deeds. Qushayrī opens here a note of loving anxiety: whoever halts at this verse finds his life troubled, for he does not know what his own hidden state is with Him — hence the verse: “I weep… and do you know what makes me weep? I weep in dread that you should part from me, sever my bond, and forsake me.”

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: He knows the hidden of the heavens and earth

mots.racine: ب ص ر
ع م ل
